mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing and compressing equity risk premiums, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the cross-sectional IC drops below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate earnings upgrades and driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when both series show positive month-over-month growth compared to periods where only one expands (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation prints above target force market pricing of prolonged hawkish monetary policy, elevating real yields and discount rates, which mechanically suppresses equity valuations and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when CPIAUCSL prints above 3.5% YoY while FEDFUNDS remains constrained below 2.0%, or the forward return distribution shows no left skew during high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth momentum serves as the primary driver of institutional earnings revisions, creating a direct positive correlation with short-term equity price discovery and forward returns.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10, or the signal produces positive returns in less than 50% of positive GDP growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, leading to immediate downward revisions in consumer discretionary earnings forecasts and driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following a >5% RSAFS decline, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production contracts while unemployment rises indicates a stagflationary supply-demand mismatch, compressing profit margins and equity multiples, which drives negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously compared to periods of rising production and stable unemployment, or the interaction term in a regression model is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth alongside a stable unemployment rate confirms economic normalization and labor market resilience, triggering risk-on institutional flows and driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive forward returns when PAYEMS adds >150k jobs and UNRATE remains below 5.0%, or the forward return median drops below zero in confirmed recovery quarters.
